description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and natural appearance. Its surface is predominantly a mix of gray, white, and blue hues, with some subtle yellowish patches, giving it a marbled look. The rock has a rough, uneven texture with visible crevices, bumps, and indentations, suggesting it has been weathered over time. One side of the rock features a visible marking or inscription, possibly a number written in black. The overall form is asymmetrical, with no flat surfaces, making it difficult to balance objects on top. The rock appears dense and solid, typical of stones found in landscaped urban environments or parks.
description: A rugged, marbled gray-blue rock with rough, uneven surfaces and visible markings. Its irregular shape and coloration suggest natural weathering and urban placement.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, featuring a visible black marking or inscription on the upper right surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rock's elongated side, with more pronounced blue and yellow marbling and deeper surface crevices.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a large, mostly gray surface with a prominent white patch and less visible markings.
description_view_3: This side displays a more triangular profile, with a mix of gray, white, and yellow tones and a smoother, less detailed surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
